Epēshu.name: Necropolis of the Founders
Epēshu.d: The first Patrin village, long since refitted as a tomb city for the founders and the notable dead. A lattice of catacombs runs beneath it; it divides Wood Quay from Whitestreet.

Epēshu.name: Wood Quay
Epēshu.d: The original port district — wooden docks, often rotted, frequented by smugglers and less successful merchants.

Epēshu.name: Whitestreet
Epēshu.d: The modern southern port, named for the blocks of marble that spill out of its warehouses.

Epēshu.name: Marble Quarter
Epēshu.d: The masonry, stonework, and sculpting workshops for which the city is famed; shops, markets, and plazas. Amat's Metalwares and the Perfumery by Chasal trade here.

Epēshu.name: Temple of Thobrauk Tegendes
Epēshu.d: The Blue Temple, seat of the Lektān priesthood and dedicated to the city's patron — the Pilot, god of seas and storms. The docks about it are kept in good repair.

Epēshu.name: Temple of the Ēkromatkar
Epēshu.d: A gilded temple run by the cult of Palagon Patermāgor, who unified the Patrin Empire.

Epēshu.name: Epēshīn Forum
Epēshu.d: The heart of the city, where governance, business, and law are debated. Gimīlahar Restored — the marble empress — overlooks it.

Epēshu.name: The Māmban
Epēshu.d: A vast garden of exotic and rare plants. The city granaries are held here.

Epēshu.name: The Relkhonak
Epēshu.d: The palatial villa of the Surīhurūk. Hethel of the Mālaz resides here — and rarely takes private audiences.

Epēshu.name: Thūkīn Amphitheatre
Epēshu.d: Gladiatorial bouts, demonstrations of magic, plays, and spectacles. This year's Games are themed on the Far South — jungle, traps, and pudkhars.

Epēshu.name: Outwall
Epēshu.d: A downtrodden slum that stinks of pigsties and stables.

Epēshu.name: Eelwynd
Epēshu.d: A coastal neighborhood known for its fish markets and violent reputation.

Epēshu.name: Kohabron
Epēshu.d: Once a separate town, swallowed as the Māgos grew; its denizens keep a strong identity of their own.

Epēshu.name: Black Barghest Inn
Epēshu.d: A large inn and gathering place for the poorer parts of the city, in Old Quarry Lot's north. Folk have been disappearing hereabouts.

Epēshu.name: Road That Walks with the Sun
Epēshu.d: The great imperial highway. It passes through the Forum before turning east to Tamaron.

Epēshu.name: Old Quarry Lot
Epēshu.d: Stripped bare of marble long ago; few trees or plants grow. The site of the mysterious disappearances.

Epēshu.name: Mount Mastery
Epēshu.d: The tallest hill overlooking the harbor, planted with rows of trophies from the city's past victories.

Epēshu.name: Bangeph Rock
Epēshu.d: A tall, smooth tine of stone jutting from the center of the bay. Vultures circle it.

Epēshu.name: The Fairy Arch
Epēshu.d: Two perpendicular arches intersecting at their peaks, deep in the woods. None know who built it, or why.
